vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or dormant. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely compromised.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil; no surface growth detected.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot shows no evidence of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains a single, struggling stem/leaf structure with white debris (eggshells) present on the soil surface.
plant_audit.P4: No distinct specimen identified in the shared black pot.

biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of white, crushed material (likely eggshells) in P3, acting as a soil amendment or pest deterrent.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-1: Minimal change; persistent lack of visible vegetative growth across all pots except for the singular P3 structure.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Static state; no new growth or further degradation observed in the last 24 hours.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of extreme decline. The lack of foliage suggests either severe dormancy, root rot, or total specimen failure. The P3 specimen is the only remaining indicator of life, though it appears stunted.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of white, crushed eggshells in P3; absence of expected plant mass in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently failing. The registry indicates four distinct plants, but the visual audit reveals a near-total loss of biomass. The yellow pot (P1) and the black pots (P2/P4) are effectively barren. P3 retains a single, fragile structure. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil enrichment or pest control, but the current state indicates the biome is not thriving under the current lighting and maintenance regime.
